mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods found in this batch. Batch 13 contains page objects for notifications, OSS emulator, overview, plan selector, and print history. Printer enrollment functionality likely exists in helper files or API utility modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription state verification methods found in this batch. API helpers for checking subscription details likely exist in other batches containing backend utility files.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. API helpers for subscription status checks likely exist in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: While overview_page.py contains pause_plan_link locator, this is for the overview page, not for the precondition setup. The test case precondition requires pausing the plan BEFORE navigating to Print and Payment History page. The pause plan action methods and CIT modal elements are not in this batch.

matched_page_objects.class_purpose: Page object representing the Print History page where users can view their printing activity, billing history, invoices, and usage statistics. Contains locators for page elements, progress bars showing page usage, plan details card, FAQ section, billing cycle selector, print history table, and various links.
matched_page_objects.relevance_reason: This is the primary page object for the Print and Payment History page where the test case executes. The test case explicitly states 'Go to Print and Payment History page under HP Instant Ink' in steps 1 and 4. This page object contains locators for the billing cycle period card elements, progress bars (plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, additional pages), page usage text elements, and billing information that the test case verifies.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Main print history card container displaying usage and billing information. This is likely the Billing Cycle Period card mentioned in the test case.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case steps 2 and 5 say 'Check the Billing Cycle Period card'. This locator targets the main print history card which is the container for the billing cycle period information.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Progress bar showing plan pages used out of monthly allocation. This is the complimentary pages progress bar.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 6 says 'Check the progress bar' and expected result says 'Verify the Complimentary pages progress bar is displayed well'. Step 11 verifies the progress bar is filled with black color. Step 18 verifies the progress bar is full filled. This locator targets the plan/complimentary pages progress bar.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Progress bar showing rollover pages used from previous cycles.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: While not explicitly mentioned in the test case, rollover pages are part of the page usage tracking system. This progress bar may be visible alongside complimentary and additional pages bars.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Message text about additional pages purchased beyond the plan.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 13 verifies 'progress bars for Complimentary pages and Additional pages are displayed well'. Step 14 checks the Additional pages progress bar details. Step 17 checks the message below Additional pages. This locator targets the additional pages message text.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Element displaying the total number of pages printed in the billing cycle.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 19 says 'Check total pages printed info' and expected result says 'Verify correct number (such as 15 pages) displayed according to the printed pages'. This locator targets the total pages printed display element.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Text values within the plan pages progress bar showing usage numbers like '0 of 10 used' or '6 of 10 used'.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 8 says 'Check the Complimentary pages value' and expected result says 'Verify the value is displayed as 0 of 10(Pause Plan) used'. Step 11 verifies '6 of 10 used'. Step 18 verifies '10 of 10 used'. This locator targets the text values within the plan pages progress bar.
Part of the page usage tracking system. May be used to verify rollover pages are not displayed or are zero during pause plan.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Text displaying additional pages purchased count like '5 of 10 used'.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 14 says 'Check the Additional pages progress bar' and expected result says 'Verify the progress bar is filled with the Yellow color and the value is displayed correctly (such as 5 of 10 used)'. This locator targets the additional pages value text.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Billing cycle selector buttons in the print history chart for selecting different billing periods.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: The test case involves viewing billing cycle period information. This selector allows switching between different billing cycles to view historical data.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Input field displaying the selected billing cycle period.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Displays the current billing cycle period being viewed. Useful for verifying which billing cycle's data is being displayed.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Text describing the plan and its price.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case step 9 says 'Check the message below Complimentary pages' and expected result says 'Verify the information with correct plan info is displayed well'. This locator may contain plan information displayed below the complimentary pages section.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Message displaying information about free trial months.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Related to subscription state. The test case precondition requires no free months, so this element should not be displayed or should show zero free months.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Main section container for print history content.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case steps 1 and 4 navigate to the Print and Payment History page. This locator targets the main section container that can be used to verify the page loaded successfully.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Page title element displaying 'Print History' or similar heading.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Test case steps 1 and 4 navigate to the Print and Payment History page. This page title can be used to verify successful navigation to the correct page.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Card displaying billing summary and plan details.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Contains billing summary and plan details that may be relevant for verifying plan information and billing cycle data.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the update plan link within the FAQ section. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.update_plan_link selector to create a locator for the change plan link in the FAQ. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: While not directly used in the test case steps, this provides access to plan management functionality from the print history page. May be useful for related test scenarios.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the plan price description text element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.plan_price_text selector to create a locator for the plan description element that displays plan pricing information. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Test case step 9 verifies 'the information with correct plan info is displayed well' below the Complimentary pages section. This property provides convenient access to the plan price/description text for verification.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: Batch 13 contains the PrintHistoryPage page object which is the primary page for this test case. Found 15 relevant locators for progress bars, page usage text elements, billing cycle card, and total pages printed. Found 2 property methods for accessing plan price text and FAQ update plan link. COVERED: Page object for Print and Payment History page, locators for complimentary pages progress bar and text values, additional pages text and message, total pages printed, billing cycle card container, page title and section. PARTIALLY COVERED: Progress bar color verification (locators found but CSS property checking methods not in this batch), additional pages progress bar (message locator found but not specific progress bar element), info icons and tooltips (not found), page refresh method (not in this batch). NOT FOUND: Precondition setup methods (printer enrollment, subscription state verification, pause plan action), backend operations (time shift, billing charge trigger, print simulation), navigation methods, info icon and tooltip locators, device type configuration, visual testing utilities, responsive testing utilities. These missing components likely exist in other batches containing helper files, API utilities, base page methods, and configuration files.
